Tina Elaine Chin
E-mail:  tina@molsci.org
Website: http://sequence.molsci.com/~tina/
 
 

Education:  B. S. Microbiology, June 1992
 University of California, Davis
 

Experience:

Senior Research Technician July 1998 to present
The Molecular Sciences Institute
Berkeley, CA

Accomplishments include:
- Engineering of vectors pJG 4-5 and pEG 202 from the two-hybrid system and developing protocols in order to clone DNA inserts by genetic recombination.
- Gene deletions in Saccharomyces cerevisiae.

Responsibilities include:
- Lab manager and safety officer of the labs of Sydney Brenner and Roger Brent
- Supervisor of two lab technicians and one lab assistant
- Ordering of laboratory reagents and supplies, maintenance of laboratory equipment, and  selection and purchase of laboratory capital equipment
 

Assistant Specialist II  June 1992 to June 1998
Immunochemistry Facility, College of Natural Resources
University of California, Berkeley, CA

Accomplishments include:
- Immunization, testing, fusion, screening and development of six monoclonal antibodies able to  detect imidazolinone herbicides in enzyme immunoassays (EIA).
- Cloning of an anti-imidazolinone antibody heavy and light chain genes to produce a  recombinant  single-chain Fv (scFv) antibody.
- Identification of the anti-imidazolinone scFvs combining site through computer homology  modeling using a database of antibody crystallographic structures in collaboration with Dr.  Victoria Roberts, Scripps Research Institute, La Jolla, CA.
- Validation of the antibody model by in vitro  site-directed mutagenesis.
- Conversion of the scFv to a disulfide-stabilized Fv (dsFv) by mutagenesis.
- Lab Safety and Radiation Compliance Officer (6/93 - present).
 
 
 

 Food Safety Research Apprentice  July, 1988 to September, 1988
United States Department of Agriculture
Agricultural Research Service, Albany , CA

Responsibilities include:
- Immunoassay of soybean protease inhibitors.
- Development of model system for studying senescence of plants.
- Test of hypothesis that protease inhibitors may be involved in plant senescence.
 

Laboratory techniques and experience:

- Tissue culture
- Mouse immunizations and tail bleeds
- Ascites production, polyclonal and monoclonal, and IgG purification
- PAGE and Western blots
- Production and purification of protein lysates using affinity columns
 Recombinant antibody and DNA techniques-- use of the Mouse ScFv Module/Recombinant Phage Antibody System (Pharmacia), vector design, cloning, restriction digests, transformation, transfection, electroporation, PCR, gel agarose electrophoresis, first-strand cDNA synthesis, DNA purification, and sequence analysis
- Dideoxy-DNA sequencing using 35S-dATP
- Hybridoma production by electrofusion
- Primer design, manual and computer aided
- Design of mutagenesis methods and protocols and use of commercial mutagenesis kits-- Chameleon and Quik-Change (Stratagene)
- Engineering of affinity tags onto scFv antibody
- Computer homology modeling using Insight on a SGI workstation

- Instructing lab techniques and computer skills to research fellows and technicians
- Writing and editing on, and preparation of graphs and computer graphics for progress reports,  grant proposals and scientific publications
- Presentation of results at scientific meetings (see publication list)
- Yeast competent and whole cell extract preparations, and transformations
- Preparation of competent and electrocompetent cells with transformation efficiences of
 1 X 1010 /µg DNA
- Proficient in using ABI sequencer and DNA/RNA synthesizer
 

 Computer Skills:

Proficient in Macintosh computers and UNIX operating systems
Competent in the following programs:
- INSIGHT (UNIX)-- three dimensional molecular display program
- AbM (UNIX)--  antibody modeling program
- HOMOLOGY (UNIX)-- program module on INSIGHT program to do antibody modeling
- MacVector (Macintosh)-- sequence analyzing program
- Vector NTI and DNAstar (Macintosh)-- sequence and oligonucleotide analyzing program
- Plasmid Artist (Macintosh)-- graphics program for vector construction
- RightPrimer (Macintosh)-- oligonucleotide analyzing program
- Microsoft Word (Macintosh)-- word processing program
- Microsoft Excel (Macintosh)-- spreadsheet program
- Passage (Macintosh)-- statistical and graphing program
- Superpaint (Macintosh)-- graphics program
- Delta Graph (Macintosh)-- graphing program
- Cricket Graph (Macintosh)-- graphing program
 
 

Publications, Presentations, Abstracts and Disclosures:

2000   Chin, T. E., C. W. Bell, R. B. Wong, and A. E. Karu.  Construction and properties  of a recombinant disulfide-stabilized Fv antibody specific for the herbicide imazethapyr, in preparation.

2000   Chin, T. E., C. W. Bell, V. A. Roberts, R. B. Wong, and A. E. Karu. Structure and mutational analysis of an antibody binding site for imidazolinone herbicides, in preparation.

2000   Chin, T. E., C. W. Bell, R. B. Wong, and A. E. Karu. Derivation and properties of a single-chain Fv antibody to imidazolinone herbicides, in preparation.

2000   Chin, T. E., R. B. Wong, J. Pont and A. E. Karu. Haptens and monoclonal antibodies for immunoassay of imidazolinone herbicides, in preparation.

1995   Chin, T. E., C. W. Bell, V. A. Roberts, R. B. Wong and A. E. Karu. Derivation of a single-chain Fv antibody to the imidazolinone herbicide imazethapyr, and modeling of the combining site.  Invited paper #364 presented by T. Chin, Antibody Engineering Symposium, International Chemical Congress of Pacific Basin Societies, Honolulu HI (Dec. 1995)

1995   Karu, A. E., C. W. Bell and T. E. Chin. Derivation of functional recombinant antibodies to small molecules: current status and future prospects.  Invited paper #76 presented by A. Karu, Symposium on Immunochemical Methods for Residue Analysis, International Chemical Congress of Pacific Basin Societies, Honolulu HI (Dec. 1995)

1995   Karu, A. E., C. W. Bell and T. E. Chin. Recombinant antibody technology. ILAR J., 37:3. pp. 132-141

1995   Wong, R. B., J. Pont, D. Johnson, J. Zulalian, T. E. Chin and A. E. Karu. Immunoaffinity chromatography applications in pesticide metabolism and residue analyses.  pp. 235-247 In:  Immunoanalysis of Agrochemicals: Emerging Technologies, J. Nelson, A. E. Karu and R. Wong (ed.) American Chemical Society, Washington D.C.

1994   Karu, A. E., T. E. Chin and C. W. Bell. Recombinant antibodies specific for imidazolinone herbicides. Licensing Disclosure, University of California, Berkeley (Dec. 1994)

1993   Karu, A. E., R. Wong, J. Pont and T. E. Chin. Monoclonal antibodies, haptens, and immunoassays for detection of imidazolinone herbicides. Licensing Disclosure, Univ. of Calif., Berkeley, (Oct. 1993)

1993   Karu, A. E., T. E. Chin, R. Wong and J. Pont. Use of multivariate statistics and neural networks to identify and quantify imidazolinone herbicides. Seminar, American Cyanamid Agrochemical Research Div., Princeton, New Jersey, October 5, 1993
